DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was uncontested. CROSS DEFENDANTS RON BORETA, JOHN BORETA, CRAIG BORETA, DAVID FREAR, FLAMINGO PARADISE PARTNERS, AND ARACELI LUGO, AS THE SPECIAL ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF VASO BORETA AND AS SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE VASO BORETA 2000 TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION TO STRIKE is overruled. The Second Amended Cross-Complaint ("SACC") adequately alleges facts that Cross-Plaintiffs performed their obligations under the oral agreements. "Such conduct satisfies the evidentiary function of the statute of frauds by confirming that a bargain was in fact reached" and as a result, the statute of frauds is not a bar to the breach of contract causes of action. (In re Marriage of Benson (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1096, 1108-1109.) The SACC is not so uncertain that the Cross-Defendants cannot reasonably respond. The request to strike the term "Boreta" from the SACC is denied. It is presumptively within Cross-Defendants' knowledge which Cross-Defendants entered into agreements with Cross-Complainants. Further, any alleged ambiguities arising from the use of the term "Boreta" can be clarified in discovery and/or at trial. The Court signed the order that was submitted by email. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH R. POLVERARI SR. OF THE BUSTAMANTE & GAGLIASSO LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (408) 977-1911 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT;
ATTY. STEVEN MORGER OF THE WENDEL ROSEN & DEAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (510) 834-6600 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT THE VASO BORETA 2000 TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Overruled. Nevada Revised Statute sec. 164.025 provides: "The trustee of a nontestamentary trust may after the death of the settlor of the trust cause to be published a notice'AND mail a copy of the notice to known or readily ascertainable creditors." (Emphasis added.) The Statute further provides: "[a] person having a claim'.against a settlor or the trust must file the claim within 90 days after the mailing, for those required to be mailed'." It is a factual inquiry as to whether Cross-Plaintiffs were "known or readily ascertainable creditors" entitled to mailing of the notice. Further, it is a factual inquiry as to when/if the Trust mailed Cross-Plaintiffs proper notice. As such, the Court cannot determine on demurrer whether Cross-Plaintiffs were required to submit a claim or if they failed to timely submit a claim. ANSWER TO BE FILED WITHIN 15 DAYS. NOTICE WAS WAIVED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/eg
